name: Days, Months and Seasons — Reading the Calendar
whatIsIt: Time is organised in a CALENDAR. There are 7 DAYS in a week (Monday to Sunday), about 30 days in a MONTH, and 12 MONTHS in a YEAR. The days repeat in order, months follow one another, and the year moves through SEASONS.
whyNeeded: Knowing days, months and seasons lets children plan and understand 'when': what day tomorrow is, when a birthday or festival falls, how long until an event. It turns the passing of time into something you can read and count.
realLifeUses: Knowing what day comes after today
Finding which month a birthday or festival is in
Counting how many days until a holiday
Knowing which season brings rain, cold, or heat

vocabulary.meaning: One of the seven: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
A block of about 30 days; there are 12 in a year.
A part of the year with its own weather: summer, monsoon, winter.

story.title: Counting Days to the Festival
story.text: Meena can hardly wait for the festival. 'Today is Monday — what is tomorrow, Amma?' 'Tuesday,' says Amma. 'The seven days always march in the same order.' Together they read the calendar: seven days make a week, twelve months make a year, and the seasons turn — so Meena can count exactly how many days until the festival arrives.
story.extractedProblem: Today is Monday. What day comes right after it?
story.answerInStory: Tuesday — the seven days always follow in the same order.

visual.caption: A week = 7 days in order: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun.

teachingGallery.title: Days, weeks and the calendar
teachingGallery.note: The calendar is counting in a pattern — 7 days in a week.

examples.caption: 7 days make ONE week: Mon to Sun.
The same weekday jumps by 7: 1, 8, 15, 22… next 29.
Months in order: 1=Jan, 2=Feb, 3=Mar… next is April (4).

standardMethod.summary: Use the fixed orders: 7 days repeat each week, 12 months make a year, and seasons cycle through the year.
steps: The 7 DAYS run Monday→Sunday, then repeat.
'After' a day is the next in order; 'before' is the one just past.
SEASONS (summer, monsoon, winter) cycle through the year.

alternateMethods.name: Say-the-order
alternateMethods.whenToUse: To find the day or month before/after.
steps: Recite the days (or months) in order.
The next you say is 'after'; the one just before is 'before'.

alternateMethods.name: Point-on-the-calendar
alternateMethods.whenToUse: To count days to an event.
steps: Count the boxes forward to the event.
alternateMethods.example: From the 10th to the 14th is 4 days.

workedExamples.problem: How many days are there in a week?
steps: Name them: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun.

workedExamples.problem: What day comes right after Wednesday?

commonMistakes.mistake: Getting the order of days or months muddled (Wednesday before Tuesday).
commonMistakes.fix: Days and months have a FIXED order. Recite them from the start each time: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday... to find what comes next.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving the day before when asked for the day after (or the reverse).
commonMistakes.fix: 'After' is the NEXT one in order; 'before' is the one just PAST. After Monday is Tuesday; before Monday is Sunday.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking a week has 5 days or a year has 10 months.
commonMistakes.fix: A week always has 7 days; a year always has 12 months. Count them on a calendar to be sure.

easy.q: How many days are in a week?
What day comes right after Monday?

medium.q: How many months are in a year?
What day comes right before Sunday?

challenge.q: What day comes right after Friday?
From the 8th to the 12th of a month, how many days is that?

questions.q: From the 5th to the 9th, how many days?

teachBackPrompt: Use a real calendar to teach someone at home the 7 days in order, the 12 months, and how to find the day AFTER and BEFORE today — plus which season we are in now.

revisionCard.summary: 7 DAYS in a week (Mon→Sun), 12 MONTHS in a year (Jan→Dec), and SEASONS cycle through the year. 'After' = next in order; 'before' = just past. Orders are fixed.

gameMission.title: Which Season Is It?
gameMission.brief: Read the calendar to name days, months and seasons, and find what comes before and after.

realLifeProject: On a real calendar, find today. Say what day it is, what day tomorrow will be, and count how many days until the weekend.

rememberIt.hook: 7 days a week, 12 months a year - knuckle trick for long months
rememberIt.unpack: Make a fist: knuckles = 31-day months, the dips between = 30 (or 28/29 for Feb). '30 days has September, April, June, and November.'

studentQuestions.q: How do I remember which months have 31 days?
studentQuestions.a: Make a fist - knuckles are 31-day months, the dips between are shorter. Or the rhyme '30 days has September...'
It is the leftover month - 28 days, or 29 in a leap year (every 4 years).
